name: Minhoto Portuguese
notes: Minhoto Portuguese (Baixo-Minho: Braga, Guimarães, Vizela), a northern EP dialect in Cintra's Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirão group. MODELLED FEATURES: (1) APICO-ALVEOLAR-ONLY SIBILANT SYSTEM — the archaic four-sibilant opposition is reduced to a single apical branch: graphemes ⟨s⟩/⟨ss⟩ AND ⟨c⟩(before e/i)/⟨ç⟩/⟨z⟩ all realise as apico-alveolar [s̺] (voiceless) / [z̺] (voiced), unlike the conservative four-sibilant Transmontano/Alto-Minhoto system which keeps apical ⟨s/ss⟩=[s̺] distinct from laminal ⟨c/ç/z⟩=[s] (Cintra 1971 p.93; Álvarez Pérez 2014 pp.37-38: this group 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch'). Coda ⟨s/z⟩ keep the pan-EP chiado neutralisation to [ʃ]/[ʒ]. (2) BETACISM — the /v/–/b/ opposition is neutralised to /b/, realised as stop [b] or spirant [β] (Cintra 1971 p.88; Pérez 2014 pp.35-37, categorical in the far north). Porto's tonic close-vowel diphthongisation ([e]>[je], [o]>[wo]) is a Baixo-Minho/Douro-Litoral subdivision trait and is NOT applied here (kept to pt-PT-x-porto). Vowel-reduction and uvular-coda allophones are enumerated as northern variants. Inherits pt-PT base.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Minhoto diverges mainly in vowel reduction and uvular coda realization

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

sources.author: Cintra, L. F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificação dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.notes: Foundational EP dialect classification. p.93: the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirao group reduces the archaic four-sibilant system to two apico-alveolar sibilants [s̺ z̺]; p.88: /v/-/b/ merger and apico-alveolar sibilants are diagnostic northern features.

sources.author: Álvarez Pérez, Xosé Afonso
sources.title: European Portuguese dialectal features: a comparison with Cintra's proposal
sources.notes: ALEPG-corpus quantitative reassessment. pp.37-38: Baixo-Minhotos, Durienses and Beirões 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch' (both s/ss and c/ç/z merge to apical), vs the conservative four-sibilant Transmontano/Alto-Minhoto. pp.35-37: /b/-/v/ merger extends along the coast beyond Coimbra and through the eastern districts (Guarda, Castelo Branco).

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
